status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: We need to differentiate the access controls of reading and writing operations, and we need to think about cross-entity access control. For example, if we are executing a workflow of MR jobs, which writing the timeline data of this workflow, we don't want other user to pollute the timeline data of the workflow by putting something under it.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Add entity-level access control of the timeline data for owners only

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

fields.summary: Extend access control for admin acls

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

fields.summary: Using TimelineNamespace to shield the entities of a user

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

comments.body: I uploaded a proposal of supporting more generalized ACLs for timeline data model.

I divided the work into two halves. In this Jira, I'd like to scope the work within  defining TimelineNamespace data model, reading from and writing into timeline store, making REST APIs for users to operate on the namespace and TimelineClient wrapper over the PUT method. In other word, this Jira focuses on making the new TimelineNamespace work end to end.

I'll create a follow up Jira to use TimelineNamespace to protect entities.

Comments:
   - AccessControlList is limited private and not available to other users outside of HDFS and MapReduce
   - Is there a reason for not supporting separate list of users and groups? 
   - How is a user of this API expected to append a user to a list? Does the user need to do a get and then set? Obviously, if 2 users try to this in parallel, it will not work correctly due to the inherent non-atomic nature of the webservice. 
   - putNamespace or createNamespace? Or is put meant to denote upsert behavior? How is a user meant to update the namespace with additional readers/writers?

Furthermore, it seems simpler to support a collection of users instead of expecting the user to serialize a set of users into a string. It also seems that the owner field allows a user to be defined where the user name can have spaces but the readers/writers list does not support spaces.
Hi [~zjshen], just a quick thing to check, shall we use the lock map in the existing leveldbstore here? Seems like some operations need to acquire locks?

bq. From your design doc, I think what we're proposing here is something to "partition" the domain of entities, but not enhancing identifications. Maybe we want to consider an alternative name like "domain" or "partition" here?

Talked to [~gtCarrera] offline. It seems to be a good suggestion, and we don't plan of nested namespaces actually. Change it to domain in the new patch.

bq. This is significantly different to any other fields. Are there any specific considerations behind this?

I follow the way that we put the start time and the insert time of an entity. It doesn't make much difference to split them and associate them with different keys, and they are usually retrieved together. I added more code comments to describe it.

bq. I think this is left out for some reasons, and maybe in YARN-2446 you're addressing this?

Yes, the use of the domain acls is in YARN-2446

bq. Shall we add a default branch here to track any potential problems? 

Added an else block here.

bq. just a quick thing to check, shall we use the lock map in the existing leveldbstore here? Seems like some operations need to acquire locks?

According to the offline discussion, the currently lock have some obvious issues. Let's fix it in a separate Jira.

Hi [~zjshen], thanks for the update. Patch LGTM now. I'd like to +1 but you may want to get another +1 from a committer as well?
Thanks [~gtCarrera9] for the review. [~zjshen], I will review your patch and provide feedback soon.

Given [~gtCarrera9] did detailed review, I quickly glossed over the patch. Mostly looks good. As I understand the patch, this is only introducing the concept of domains for ACLs, and adding APIs to create domains.

Quick comments 
 - TimelineDataManager.getNamspace() to be renamed to refer to domains.
 - TimelineClientImpl is printing errors to stdout. Can we instead use a logger?
[~vinodkv], thanks for the comments. I've addressed them in the new patch. In addition, I've fixed a client bug together.

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

comments.body: I uploaded a proposal of supporting more generalized ACLs for timeline data model.

I divided the work into two halves. In this Jira, I'd like to scope the work within  defining TimelineNamespace data model, reading from and writing into timeline store, making REST APIs for users to operate on the namespace and TimelineClient wrapper over the PUT method. In other word, this Jira focuses on making the new TimelineNamespace work end to end.

I'll create a follow up Jira to use TimelineNamespace to protect entities.

Comments:
   - AccessControlList is limited private and not available to other users outside of HDFS and MapReduce
   - Is there a reason for not supporting separate list of users and groups? 
   - How is a user of this API expected to append a user to a list? Does the user need to do a get and then set? Obviously, if 2 users try to this in parallel, it will not work correctly due to the inherent non-atomic nature of the webservice. 
   - putNamespace or createNamespace? Or is put meant to denote upsert behavior? How is a user meant to update the namespace with additional readers/writers?

Furthermore, it seems simpler to support a collection of users instead of expecting the user to serialize a set of users into a string. It also seems that the owner field allows a user to be defined where the user name can have spaces but the readers/writers list does not support spaces.
Hi [~zjshen], just a quick thing to check, shall we use the lock map in the existing leveldbstore here? Seems like some operations need to acquire locks?

bq. From your design doc, I think what we're proposing here is something to "partition" the domain of entities, but not enhancing identifications. Maybe we want to consider an alternative name like "domain" or "partition" here?

Talked to [~gtCarrera] offline. It seems to be a good suggestion, and we don't plan of nested namespaces actually. Change it to domain in the new patch.

bq. This is significantly different to any other fields. Are there any specific considerations behind this?

I follow the way that we put the start time and the insert time of an entity. It doesn't make much difference to split them and associate them with different keys, and they are usually retrieved together. I added more code comments to describe it.

bq. I think this is left out for some reasons, and maybe in YARN-2446 you're addressing this?

Yes, the use of the domain acls is in YARN-2446

bq. Shall we add a default branch here to track any potential problems? 

Added an else block here.

bq. just a quick thing to check, shall we use the lock map in the existing leveldbstore here? Seems like some operations need to acquire locks?

According to the offline discussion, the currently lock have some obvious issues. Let's fix it in a separate Jira.

Hi [~zjshen], thanks for the update. Patch LGTM now. I'd like to +1 but you may want to get another +1 from a committer as well?
Thanks [~gtCarrera9] for the review. [~zjshen], I will review your patch and provide feedback soon.

Given [~gtCarrera9] did detailed review, I quickly glossed over the patch. Mostly looks good. As I understand the patch, this is only introducing the concept of domains for ACLs, and adding APIs to create domains.

Quick comments 
 - TimelineDataManager.getNamspace() to be renamed to refer to domains.
 - TimelineClientImpl is printing errors to stdout. Can we instead use a logger?
[~vinodkv], thanks for the comments. I've addressed them in the new patch. In addition, I've fixed a client bug together.
